channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House Republicans unveil bill to avoid shutdown and they're daring Democrats to oppose it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans unveiled a spending bill Saturday that would keep federal agencies funded through Sept. 30, pushing ahead with a go-it-alone strategy that seems certain to spark a major confrontation with Democrats over the contours of government spending.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FEMA cancels classes at national fire training academy amid federal funding cuts
item.description: The National Fire Academy, the country's preeminent federal fire training academy, has canceled classes effective immediately amid the ongoing flurry of funding freezes and staffing cuts by President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Al Green censure highlights increased appetite for retribution in House
item.description: A formal House reprimand didn't contain the fallout from Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) interrupting President Trump’s speech to Congress, showing how petty fights and demands for retribution have become an increasingly prominent part of business on the House floor.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP campaign chairman says voters will 'reward us' for the Trump-Musk DOGE cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Almost two months into President Donald Trump's second term, the chairman of the House Republican campaign committee is already predicting his party will pick up seats in the midterm elections some 20 months away. Rep. Richard Hudson, R-N.C.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's what happened during President Donald Trump's 7th week in office
item.description: Trump launched the week telling Americans that his administration would work to take "America’s destiny into our own hands" and vowed that "this will be our greatest era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US economic worries mount as Trump implements tariffs, cuts workforce and freezes spending
item.description: President Donald Trump's flurry of tariffs, government layoffs and spending freezes may be doing more to harm the U.S. economy than to fix it.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tribes and Native American students sue over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: Three tribal nations and five Native American students say in a lawsuit that the Trump administration has failed its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools.
item.dc:creator: Graham Lee Brewer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump order denies student loan relief to nonprofit workers engaged in 'improper' activity
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is ordering changes to the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program that would disqualify workers of nonprofit groups deemed to have engaged in “improper” activities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former illegal immigrant aide to AOC self-deports to Colombia amid questions about employment: report
item.description: A former aide to Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., has self-deported after living in the United States illegally since his childhood.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican lawmakers hold telephone town halls as protesters demand more
item.description: HOLLAND, Mich. (AP) — Protesters demanding an in-person town hall from their western Michigan GOP congressman chanted loudly Friday as honking drivers signaled support, disrupting the usual calm of a conservative family vacation town. Mere hours later, Rep. Bill Huizenga held a town hall — by phone.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Democrats ask Rubio for answers on now-canceled $400 million Tesla plan
item.description: A letter from two House Democrats presses Rubio for details about who approved an effort to try to use hundreds of millions of dollars of taxpayer money on armored electric vehicles from Tesla.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump created a task force Friday to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe’s premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when Trump’s on-again-off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Minnesota among states that sued Trump administration over federal workers' mass firings
item.description: The lawsuit says the mass firings will cause irreparable burdens and expenses on the states, because they will have to support recently unemployed workers and adjudicate claims of unemployment assistance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: 'Final Moments' With Iran
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics conten
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wisconsin Democrats vent, cry and issue call to action to counter Musk and Trump
item.description: SAUK CITY, Wis. (AP) — They came to vent and to cry and to exhort. The overflow crowd of frustrated Democrats who met this week in a small Wisconsin city's library voiced anger over President Donald Trump, his billionaire adviser Elon Musk and the direction of the country.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Targeted Tesla attacks continue amid outrage at Elon Musk
item.description: Some Tesla showrooms, dealerships, and charging stations have been set on fire or vandalized in other ways, ostensibly in protest of Musk's role.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: Housing policies cited as major reason families forced to give up pets
item.description: Pet rescues and animal shelters hear it over and over: a family has to give up their dog because they had to move and the new place has a rule.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate Democrats file complaint against DC US attorney Ed Martin
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee have filed a formal complaint against District of Columbia U.S. attorney Ed Martin with the D.C.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: War heroes and military firsts are among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — References to a World War II Medal of Honor recipient, the Enola Gay aircraft that dropped an atomic bomb on Japan and the first women to pass Marine infantry training are among the tens of thousands of photos and online posts marked for deletion as the Defense Department works to
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, LOLITA C. BALDOR and KEVIN VINEYS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order on student loan forgiveness program
item.description: As part of the program, those working for the government or a non-profit organization have their loans forgiven.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: A judge in the nation's capital declines to block DOGE from Treasury systems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge in Washington refused Friday to block employees of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury systems containing sensitive personal data for millions of Americans. She acknowledged, however, privacy concerns about their work.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New fines and awareness help reduce litter on Minnesota lakes, but advocates want more action
item.description: Lake advocates say a recent state law is helping curb the problem of trash left on Minnesota’s frozen lakes. But they're hoping for more action, including passage of a bill requiring state agencies establish a statewide “Keep It Clean” program and provide funding for local cleanup efforts.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Judge declines to immediately spare USAID contractors from firings
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday declined to immediately spare U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) contractors from mass firings, letting move forward a core part of the Trump administration’s effort to dismantle the agency. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Hunter Biden asks for laptop data case to be dismissed, citing financial problems
item.description: “Plaintiff has suffered a significant downturn in his income and has significant debt in the millions of dollars range,” Hunter Biden’s attorneys wrote in a court filing.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: GOP lawmakers balk at Trump’s call to repeal CHIPS Act
item.description: Republican lawmakers on Wednesday said President Trump’s call for Congress to “get rid of” the 2022 CHIPS and Science Act, which provided $52 billion for the domestic semiconductor manufacturing industry, is dead on arrival on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Proposal would use cameras to ticket double parking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- A proposed pilot program aims to crack down on various curb violations, including drivers who double park in New York City. The program would use cameras to issue automatic tickets.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Dhillon, PIX 11

item.title: DeSantis' State of the State: government accountability a key focus
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis gave his state of the state address Tuesday and offered his plan for the year ahead. Members of the legislature, and leaders past and present gathered in Tallahassee for the speech Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 2 Las Vegas teens present antisemitism bill at Nevada Legislature in Carson City
item.description: Two Las Vegas high school students helped put antisemitism in the public eye on Monday as they testified at the Nevada Legislature on one bill and helped present another.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration to downsize Social Security Administration
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Elon Musk, the world's wealthiest person, who is also a senior advisor to President Donald Trump, is now setting his sights in recent comments on the Social Security Administration (SSA). "Social Security is the biggest Ponzi scheme of all time," said Musk.
item.dc:creator: Jay Dow, PIX 11
